sections.paragraph:
τρυφή, ῆς, ἡ (θρύπτω; Eur., X., Pla.+; SIG 888, 124; LXX; OdeSol 11:16a, 24; Test12Patr; JosAs 16:8; ApcSed 11:11 p. 134, 27 Ja.; Mel., P. 18, 126)

① engagement in a fast, self-indulgent lifestyle, indulgence, reveling (Sextus 73; Philo, Spec. Leg. 2, 240, Somn. 1, 123; TestJos 3:4; TestBenj 6:3; Jos., Ant. 10, 193; 16, 301) 2 Pt 2:13 (cp. Cicero, Pro Caelio 47); Hs 6, 4, 4ab; 6, 5, 1; 3; 4; 5. τρυφὴ πονηρά Hm 8:3. ἄγγελος τρυφῆς Hs 6, 2, 1. Pl. (Jos., Vi. 284; ApcSed 11:11) Hm 6, 2, 5; 11:12; 12, 2, 1; Hs 6, 2, 2; 4; 6, 5, 6; 7c. AcPl Ha 2, 19 (s. πολῖτις).

② a state of extraordinary ease and comfort, luxury, splendor (Ps.-Lucian, Amor. 3 ἐσθὴς μέχρι ποδῶν τὴν τρυφὴν καθειμένη) Lk 7:25.

③ a state of intense satisfaction, enjoyment, joy, delight, ext. of 1 in a good sense (cp. τρυφάω end) (Menand., Cith. Fgm. 5, 2 J.; SEG VIII, 549, 28 bestowed by Isis; En 14:13; TestJud 25:2; JosAs 16:8; Philo, Cher. 12; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 228; Mel., P. 18, 126) οἵαν τρυφὴν ἔχει ἡ μέλλουσα ἐπαγγελία what enjoyment the promise of the future brings 2 Cl 10:4. παράδεισος τρυφῆς a Paradise of delight Dg 12:1 (Gen 3:23; OdeSol 11:24).—Reveling in the doing of good Hs 6, 5, 7b; ibid. a, the pl.—DELG s.v. θρύπτω. M-M.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

OdeSol OdeSol = Gk. Ode of Solomon 11: PBodmer XI; III a.d.—List 2

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

ApcSed ApcSed = Apocalypse of Sedrach—List 2

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Sextus Sextus , end of II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestBenj TestBenj = Testament of Benjamin, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Hm Hm = Mandates

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

Ps.-Lucian Ps.-Lucian , after II a.d.—List 5

ext. ext. = extension

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

ibid. ibid. = ibidem (in the same book or passage)

  1. pl. = plural

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1018
